2606.23124.title: PRIDE: Privileged Information-enhanced Distillation for Empathetic Dialogue Generation
2606.23124.paper_summary: Large language models have demonstrated significant capabilities in generating diverse and context-aware responses for empathetic dialogue. However, their computational demands severely limit their deployment in resource-constrained environments. While knowledge distillation offers a promising compression solution, it often fails to transfer the nuanced understanding essential for empathy, as it overlooks the implicit contextual cues that guide human connection. To bridge this gap, we propose a \textbf{pr}ivileged \textbf{i}nformation-enhanced knowledge \textbf{d}istillation method for \textbf{e}mpathetic dialogue generation (PRIDE). Our method leverages privileged information, such as expert psychological annotations or future event summaries, which is available exclusively during training but unavailable at inference time. This allows us to transfer the teacher model's empathetic reasoning to smaller models without relying on extra inputs during deployment. Specifically, PRIDE has three key components: (1) An empathy-reasoning prompt that guides the teacher to explicitly decompose the empathetic process into understanding feelings and analyzing situations step-by-step; (2) A multi-source attention mechanism that directs the student to effectively integrate privileged information; (3) A dual-alignment loss that combines reversed Kullback-Leibler divergence and maximum mean discrepancy to ensure robust knowledge transfer at both logit and feature levels. Experiments on multi-modal and text-only datasets demonstrate that our method achieves competitive performance, and in some cases matches or even surpasses larger teacher models in terms of accuracy and semantic relevance.
2606.23124.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型在生成多樣且具情境意識的同理心對話方面展現了顯著的能力。<lb/>然而，它們的計算需求嚴重限制了在資源受限環境中的部署。<lb/>雖然知識蒸餾提供了一個有前景的壓縮解決方案，但它往往無法轉移對同理心至關重要的細微理解，因為它忽略了指導人際連結的隱含情境線索。<lb/>為了彌補這一差距，我們提出了一種\textbf{pr}ivileged \textbf{i}nformation增強的知識\textbf{d}istillation方法，用於\textbf{e}mpathetic對話生成（PRIDE）。<lb/>我們的方法利用特權信息，例如專家心理註釋或未來事件摘要，這些信息在訓練期間專門可用，但在推理時不可用。<lb/>這使我們能夠將教師模型的同理心推理轉移到較小的模型上，而無需在部署期間依賴額外的輸入。<lb/>具體而言，PRIDE有三個關鍵組件：（1）一個同理心推理提示，指導教師明確地將同理心過程分解為理解感受和逐步分析情境；（2）一個多源注意機制，指導學生有效整合特權信息；（3）一個雙重對齊損失，結合反向Kullback-Leibler散度和最大均值差異，以確保在logit和特徵層面上穩健的知識轉移。<lb/>在多模態和僅文本數據集上的實驗表明，我們的方法達到了競爭性的性能，在某些情況下在準確性和語義相關性方面甚至與較大的教師模型相匹配或超越。
2606.23124.author: Jiaqiang Wu et.al.
2606.23124.authors: Jiaqiang Wu, Zhouan Zhu, Shangfei Wang
